container_template.readme:
# Qwen3.5-9B with SGLang

This deployment runs `Qwen3.5-9B` with the official `SGLang` runtime and exposes an OpenAI-compatible API for tools such as OpenClaw, OpenCode, and other compatible clients.

<Callout variation="note">This recipe is public by default. If you enabled Container Gateway Authentication during deployment, include `Salad-Api-Key` in your requests.</Callout>

## Use With OpenClaw

If you want to connect this deployment to OpenClaw, follow this guide:

- [Use OpenClaw with a Salad-hosted LLM](https://docs.salad.com/container-engine/how-to-guides/openclaw/openclaw-ollama-salad-hosted-telegram)

## Access

- <Link url={`https://${props.networking.dns}/health`}>Health Check</Link>
- <Link url={`https://${props.networking.dns}/v1/models`}>List Models</Link>


This recipe follows the official Qwen SGLang launch pattern and keeps Qwen thinking enabled by default.

To disable thinking for a specific request, include this in the JSON body:

<CodeBlock language="json">{`"chat_template_kwargs": {"enable_thinking": false}`}</CodeBlock>

## Curl Example

<CodeBlock language="bash">{`curl https://${props.networking.dns}/v1/chat/completions \\
  -X POST \\
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\
  -d '{
    "model": "qwen3.5-9b",
    "messages": [
      {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant."},
      {"role": "user", "content": "Write a short explanation of mixture-of-experts models."}
    ],
    "temperature": 0.6,
    "top_p": 0.95,
    "top_k": 20,
    "max_tokens": 512
  }'
`}</CodeBlock>

If you enabled Container Gateway Authentication during deployment, add `-H 'Salad-Api-Key: your_salad_api_key'` to your requests.

## Resources

- [Use OpenClaw with a Salad-hosted LLM](https://docs.salad.com/container-engine/how-to-guides/openclaw/openclaw-ollama-salad-hosted-telegram)
- [Qwen3.5-9B model card](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B)
- [Qwen SGLang deployment docs](https://qwen.readthedocs.io/en/v3.0/deployment/sglang.html)
- [SGLang server arguments](https://docs.sglang.io/advanced_features/server_arguments.html)
- [Recipe Source](https://github.com/SaladTechnologies/salad-recipes/tree/master/recipes/qwen3.5-9b-sglang)
- <Link url={`https://github.com/SaladTechnologies/salad-recipes/issues/new?title=Qwen3.5-9B%20SGLang&body=Image%3A%20${props.container.image}`}>Report an Issue on GitHub</Link>

command: exec python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path "${SGLANG_MODEL_PATH}" --served-model-name "${SGLANG_SERVED_MODEL_NAME}" --host :: --port 8000 --tp-size "${SGLANG_TP_SIZE}" --mem-fraction-static "${SGLANG_MEM_FRACTION_STATIC}" --context-length "${SGLANG_CONTEXT_LENGTH}" --attention-backend triton --reasoning-parser qwen3 --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder

form.title: Qwen3.5-9B (SGLang)
form.description:
Run `Qwen3.5-9B` with `SGLang` on a Salad GPU as an OpenAI-compatible API for a wide variety of tools.

This recipe is designed to be easy easy to use:

- the model is already chosen for you
- it downloads automatically on startup
- server is configured with recommended settings for the model
- you can connect tools such as OpenClaw, OpenCode, and other OpenAI-compatible clients

Simply enter a container group name, choose whether to require a SaladCloud API Key, deploy, and wait for startup to finish.

Thinking is enabled by default. If you want a direct response for a specific request, send `chat_template_kwargs: {"enable_thinking": false}` in that request body.

This recipe follows the official Qwen SGLang launch pattern for `Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B`, including `--reasoning-parser qwen3`. It also enables `--tool-call-parser qwen3_coder` so the endpoint is ready for tool-using clients and agent frameworks.

Technical note: if you want to change the model or runtime settings later, edit these environment variables in Advanced Configuration:

- `SGLANG_MODEL_PATH`
- `SGLANG_SERVED_MODEL_NAME`
- `SGLANG_CONTEXT_LENGTH`
- `SGLANG_MEM_FRACTION_STATIC`
- `SGLANG_TP_SIZE`

Helpful references:

- [Qwen3.5-9B model card](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B)
- [Qwen SGLang deployment docs](https://qwen.readthedocs.io/en/v3.0/deployment/sglang.html)
- [SGLang server arguments](https://docs.sglang.io/advanced_features/server_arguments.html)

container_group_name.title: Container Group Name
container_group_name.description: Required* Must be 2-63 lowercase letters, numbers, or hyphens. Cannot start with a number or start or end with a hyphen.

networking_auth.title: Require Container Gateway Authentication
networking_auth.description: When enabled, requests must include a SaladCloud API Key to access the container. When disabled, public (unauthenticated) access is allowed.

short_description: Serve Qwen3.5-9B with SGLang as an OpenAI-compatible API for OpenClaw, OpenCode, and other tools.
